title: Fix admin authorization error in Admin Panel

requirements.summary: Fix the 'Unauthorized: Only admins can perform this action' error by ensuring the backend recognizes the admin session established through loginWithPassword when performing image uploads and social link updates
acceptanceCriteria: After logging in with valid admin credentials (DeepakKumawat / Kinnu*0613), uploading a logo image in the Site Images tab succeeds without showing the unauthorized error.
After logging in with valid admin credentials, uploading a cover/hero image succeeds without showing the unauthorized error.
After logging in with valid admin credentials, saving social links (WhatsApp number and Instagram URL) succeeds without showing the unauthorized error.
The unauthorized error banner does not appear in the Admin Panel for any action performed by a correctly authenticated admin session.
Unauthenticated or non-admin users still receive the unauthorized error when attempting admin actions.

file_operations.description: After successful loginWithPassword call in the admin initialization flow, ensure the actor instance is properly authenticated for subsequent admin mutations. Verify that the backend session persists across actor method calls within the same browser session.

Add error handling and retry logic for authentication failures. If uploadLogo, uploadCoverImage, uploadArtistPortrait, or updateMediaContacts mutations fail with an 'Unauthorized' error, attempt to re-authenticate using loginWithPassword with the stored credentials before retrying the operation. Display user-friendly error messages distinguishing between authorization failures and other errors.

Enhance the mutation hooks (useUploadLogo, useUploadCoverImage, useUploadArtistPortrait, useUpdateMediaContacts) to include onError handlers that detect authorization failures. When an 'Unauthorized' error occurs, trigger a silent re-authentication flow using the actor's loginWithPassword capability before invalidating queries or showing error toasts.

Add a helper function getStoredCredentials() that retrieves the admin username and password hash from the session used during login. This allows the frontend to re-authenticate with the backend if the session expires or is lost between mutations.
